improvement_directive: Prove a uniform data-only upper theorem for the same welfare-regret estimand at the corrected exponent, by adding standard primitive nuisance learnability/local-complexity conditions and deriving the clipped-score drift and weighted product bounds from them. Alternatively, prove a matching feasible minimax upper with the finite-VC logarithmic factor explicitly included, so the lower bound is paired with an attainable estimator rather than only an oracle benchmark.
critique: The delivered result is a real optimized weak-overlap two-point converse, but the headline minimax exponent is not established because there is no matching feasible full-class upper bound. The upper theorem is per-law and oracle: it assumes the true nuisance tuple is known, carries a finite-VC logarithmic factor, and does not control the unknown-nuisance clipped-score drift uniformly over the stated law class. The lower bound is also conditional on a primitive local policy-richness witness, so it is a meaningful specialized converse rather than a complete characterization of policy-regret minimax rates. Thus the note proves a useful subfield-level lower-bound/oracle-bracket result, not a field-level sharp rate or self-contained minimax theorem.
